name: Humanloop
what_it_does: LLM prompt management, evaluation, and fine-tuning platform API that enables teams to version prompts, collect human feedback, run evaluations, and fine-tune models — serving as the operational layer between LLM APIs and production applications.
use_cases: Versioning and A/B testing prompts in production without code deployments using the Prompt Management API
Collecting structured human feedback on LLM outputs to build fine-tuning datasets
Running automated evaluations combining LLM-as-judge and human annotation for quality assurance
Managing tool definitions and agent configurations centrally with version control
Fine-tuning models on curated production data captured through the logging pipeline
not_for: Low-latency proxy-based monitoring (Humanloop adds more overhead than simple proxy solutions like Helicone)
Teams that do not need prompt versioning or fine-tuning — simpler observability tools are less friction
Open-source or self-hosted deployments — Humanloop is a managed SaaS product with no self-hosting option
best_when: You have a production LLM application where prompt quality directly affects business outcomes and you need systematic version control, eval, and feedback collection to improve it over time.
avoid_when: You need a lightweight logging solution — Humanloop's feature surface (prompt management, fine-tuning, evals) adds complexity that isn't justified for simple observability use cases.

auth.notes: API key per workspace, passed as Authorization header. Keys are workspace-scoped with no operation-level granularity. Separate keys can be created per environment (dev, staging, prod).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: up to 1,000 logged datapoints/month, 2 projects, basic evals
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter: $25/month; Growth: custom pricing; Enterprise: custom with SLA
pricing.notes: Fine-tuning costs are additional and depend on model provider (OpenAI, etc.). Pricing scales with logged datapoints.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply per API key; exact limits are plan-dependent and not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with field-level validation messages. SDK surfaces errors with context about which operation failed. Webhook payloads are well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Log calls are not idempotent — duplicate submissions create multiple records. Prompt version creation is idempotent by name+version combination.
known_agent_gotchas: Prompt versions are immutable once published — agents must create new versions rather than updating existing ones, which can accumulate quickly
The SDK's log() call is async by default and may be dropped if the process exits immediately after — always await or flush
Tool schemas must match exactly between Humanloop prompt config and the agent's runtime — schema drift causes silent mismatches
Human feedback collection requires configuring feedback schemas upfront — agents cannot dynamically define new feedback types at runtime
Fine-tuning jobs are long-running (hours) — agents triggering fine-tuning should poll job status rather than awaiting synchronously

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Workspace-scoped API keys with no granular permissions. SOC 2 Type II certified. EU data residency available for GDPR compliance. No self-hosting option means all data transits Humanloop infrastructure.
